raw_request: Hi, I forgot my Windows password and the self-service portal keeps timing out. Can you help me reset it?
My corporate password expired this morning and the reset link in the email never arrived. I cannot get into Outlook or Teams for our 10am team standup.
I'm locked out of my account and I have a board demo in 30 minutes that I'm presenting from this laptop. SSO keeps rejecting my credentials and the recovery flow is failing.
The scroll wheel on my work mouse stopped responding. It's annoying but I can still get by with the touchpad.
My laptop fan has been running at full speed for the last two days and the chassis gets very hot during meetings. Performance is starting to degrade.
Rack 14 in the Milan datacenter just lost both power supplies on the primary storage array. Multiple production services are now offline and customer traffic is failing.
The new internal travel-booking app shows the wrong currency symbol next to expense totals on the summary page. Numbers look correct, just the symbol is off.
Excel keeps crashing every time I open the Q1 forecast workbook. It happens within seconds of opening and I lose any unsaved changes from the linked sheets.
Finance cannot generate the month-end reconciliation report. The button throws a 'permission denied' error and the audit deadline is tomorrow afternoon.
Please add me to the marketing-assets shared drive folder so I can pick up brand templates for next week's campaign.
Buongiorno, ho bisogno di accesso al nuovo tool di analytics 'Lumen' per il mio team di product. We start onboarding clients with it next Monday and four analysts still cannot log in.
Oncall here. I need read-write access to the production payments database for the next four hours to investigate a stuck settlement batch impacting merchant payouts.
I just got an email that looks like our IT password-reset notice but the link points to a typo'd domain. I did not click anything but wanted to flag it so you can warn other staff.
Defender just quarantined what it flagged as a credential-stealing trojan on my laptop after I opened a vendor invoice attachment. The machine is still on the corporate network.
Our SIEM is showing 8GB of outbound traffic from a finance workstation to an unknown IP overnight, and the user says they were not logged in. We suspect data exfiltration in progress.
